title: vicia americana var. oregana
intro: Vicia americana var. oregana, also known as Western vetch, is a perennial plant that belongs to the pea family Fabaceae. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. Western vetch has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, headache, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. Western vetch can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal capsules or dried as a supplement.
